content: I'm trying to create a kernel module, which register a character device in /dev, which in c should be a chrdev. I found the document here, but I cannot find the related kernel version. So what's the correct way of doing this?
You're looking at extremely old documentation. There's no way to create a character device right now.
is it possible to wrap some c bindings or use some alternative ways in rust?

Hi, I need to use chrdev functions like alloc_chrdev_region and cdev_init not miscdevice. I submitted an simple RFC patch for use it unsafe. Do you have a plan to make patches that abstraction for chrdev? It seems that there is implementation for this in old rust branch. #General > chrdev abstraction

Do you have a plan to make patches that abstraction for chrdev?

The usual kernel rule is that only APIs with users can be usptreamed, please see https://rust-for-linux.com/contributing#submitting-new-abstractions-and-modules.

Hi, I need to use chrdev functions like alloc_chrdev_region and cdev_init not miscdevice.

Why do you need multiple character device nodes for a single driver? Shouldn't you be doing "one minor number per device" which the miscdevice api should handle just fine. Do you have more specifics about the driver you are wanting to write that requires a range of character device nodes?

Actually, I'm developing some npu driver out of source tree. there is working C proprietary driver. and I wonder that it could be works in rust. It uses cdev to have multiple char device like /dev/npu/npu0, npu1, npu2, .... As @Greg KH said, it seems that it's fine to use miscdevice than cdev for now. I'll pull miscdevice branch and test it.

NPU drivers should be using the in-kernel accel framework, NOT talking directly to userspace through a custom character device. Please work on converting the existing C code to that first, before attempting a rust conversion, as that will be required to do anyway.

Do you have a link to the source for this driver anywhere online?

Greg KH said:

Please work on converting the existing C code to that first, before attempting a rust conversion, as that will be required to do anyway.

If the existing C code is done "the wrong way", and needs to be rewritten to get upstream, why not do the rewrite in Rust "the right way"?

Do you have a link to the source for this driver anywhere online?

It's not opened. I think it's not good usage that rewrite it in rust.
Ok, good luck, but please move the C code to use the existing accel api as that is going to be required no matter what language your driver is in.
